name: Opening Day Chaos: World Cup 2026 Kicks Off
angle: News-driven daily quiz, pitched for casual fans. Built on opening day of WC2026 (Mexico v South Africa). Funny/ironic spine: two famous stadiums (the Azteca and MetLife) both had their sponsor names hidden by FIFA, and Haiti were made to bin a kit over a historic battle scene.

questions.question: The 2026 World Cup kicks off today. Which country plays in the opening match?
Three countries are co-hosting the 2026 World Cup. Which three?
Mexico open the tournament today against which nation?
Who are the reigning world champions heading into 2026?
Which superstar captained Argentina to the 2022 World Cup title?
A bank paid a fortune to rename Mexico City's famous stadium 'Estadio Banorte' — but FIFA bans sponsor names at the World Cup. What is that stadium's famous original name?
FIFA made Haiti change their shirt just before the tournament because the design showed what kind of image?
How many teams are at the 2026 World Cup, the most ever?
Scotland are back at the World Cup after decades away. Famously, they have never managed to do what?

options.D: Qualify two tournaments in a row

questions.question: Where will the 2026 World Cup final be played, just outside New York?
Like the Azteca, MetLife Stadium has its name hidden by FIFA for the final. Why?

options.A: FIFA bans sponsor names at the World Cup
options.B: The stadium is still under construction
options.C: A legal dispute over the name
options.D: It is kept secret for security

questions.question: Which country has won the most World Cups, with five titles?
Which legend is the only player to win the World Cup three times?
Argentina beat which country on penalties in the 2022 World Cup final?
Players often struggle in Mexico City because its stadium sits at an extreme...?

sources: https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49026257/ (ESPN live blog, 11 June 2026 — Mexico v South Africa opener)
https://mexiconewsdaily.com/news/fifa-azteca-stadium-mexico-city-stadium/ (FIFA bans corporate stadium names; Azteca renamed for the WC)
https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2026/6/11/fifa-world-cup-2026-haiti-kit-political-flag-war-imagery-football (Haiti kit banned — Battle of Vertieres battle scene)
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_final (final at MetLife Stadium, New Jersey; FIFA hides sponsor name)
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022_FIFA_World_Cup_Final (Argentina beat France on penalties; Messi captain)
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pele (only player to win three World Cups)
